amendments.normalized_body: TaskRunEntry.milestone_id is marked Option<String> with #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] (model.rs:277-278), but the acceptance criteria states: "New fields (run_id, plan_hash, outcome_detail, milestone_id) should be required (non-optional) where they are known at write time." milestone_id is always known at write time — it's passed as a parameter to every write path. Make milestone_id a required String field. This also removes the need for backfill-on-read in FsTaskRunLineageStore::read_task_runs_from_path (fs.rs:2556-2558) and the canonicalize_entry_for_write method that patches it up.

Remove CompletionJournalDetails::parse_legacy (fs.rs:2289-2321) and the legacy-format branch in repairable_completion_event (fs.rs:2449-2459) that handles comma-delimited 'project=..., outcome=...' journal details. The acceptance criteria states: 'Remove any legacy parsing, backfill-on-read, or repair paths that only exist to handle a hypothetical migration from an older schema.' Since there is no existing milestone data on disk, the only journal details format is the structured JSON written by render_completion_journal_details. The parse_legacy path adds dead code and masks serialization bugs.

Remove the backward-compat deserialization test task_run_entry_backward_compat_without_new_fields (service.rs:1232-1245) and the legacy-backfill test read_task_runs_backfills_milestone_id_for_legacy_entries (service.rs:1247-1282). These tests validate migration behavior from an older schema that the acceptance criteria says does not exist. Also remove progress_deserialization_defaults_skipped_beads (model.rs test) if skipped_beads is made a required field (removing its #[serde(default)] annotation at model.rs:204).

Remove the unused _started_at parameter from find_matching_running_task_run (model.rs:421). The parameter is prefixed with _ to suppress the dead-code warning but is never referenced in the function body. Update the single call site in FsTaskRunLineageStore::record_task_run_start (fs.rs:2815-2821) to stop passing it.

Remove the legacy-schema compatibility and repair paths; they directly contradict the task contract and weaken validation. `TaskRunEntry` still accepts missing linkage metadata through optional/defaulted fields (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:275-298`), `read_task_runs_from_path` silently backfills a missing `milestone_id` instead of rejecting the row (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2536-2558`), and completion journal parsing/repair still accepts and rewrites legacy delimited payloads (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2283-2313`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:2424-2459`). The prompt explicitly says there is no old on-disk data and these migration/backfill paths should be removed, so shipping them means malformed new writes will be hidden instead of failing fast.

Fix start-attempt matching so it distinguishes a real retry from an idempotent replay. `find_matching_running_task_run` ignores its `started_at` parameter entirely (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:416-442`), and `record_task_run_start` then either reuses any sole runless open row or appends a second row when the retry arrives with a newly known `run_id` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2815-2856`). That makes the audit trail wrong in both directions: a genuine second retry without `run_id` is silently collapsed into the first attempt, while a replay of the same logical attempt that now has a `run_id` leaves behind a phantom `Running` row forever. The current behavior is even codified in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3179-3235`.

Fix start-attempt matching so it distinguishes a real retry from an idempotent replay. `find_matching_running_task_run` ignores its `started_at` parameter entirely (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:416-442`), and `record_task_run_start` then either reuses any sole runless open row or appends a second row when the retry arrives with a newly known `run_id` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2815-2856`). payload.summary: Final review accepted 4 amendment(s); restart required.
